functions.name: synphot.specio.read_ascii_spec
documentation:
Read ASCII spectrum.

ASCII table must have following columns:

> 1. Wavelength data
> 2. Flux data

It can have more than 2 columns but the rest is ignored.
Comments are discarded.

# Parameters

filename

: Spectrum file name or pointer.

wave_unit, flux_unit

: Wavelength and flux units, which default to Angstrom and FLAM,
  respectively.

kwargs

: Keywords accepted by {func}`astropy.io.ascii.read`.

# Returns

header

: This is just an empty dictionary, so returned values
  are the same as {func}`read_fits_spec`.

wavelengths, fluxes

: Wavelength and flux of the spectrum.
  They are set to 'float64' percision.

functions.name: synphot.specio.read_fits_spec
documentation:
Read FITS spectrum.

Wavelength and flux units are extracted from respective `TUNITn`
keywords, from data table (not primary) header.
If these keywords are not present, units are taken from
`wave_unit` and `flux_unit` instead.

# Parameters

filename

: Spectrum file name or pointer.

ext: int

: FITS extension with table data. Default is 1.

wave_col, flux_col

: Wavelength and flux column names (case-insensitive).

wave_unit, flux_unit

: Wavelength and flux units. These are *no longer used*.
  Define your units in the respective `TUNITn`
  keywords in table (not primary) header.

  :::{deprecated} 1.4
  :::

# Returns

header

: Primary header only. Extension header is discarded.

wavelengths, fluxes

: Wavelength and flux of the spectrum.

functions.name: synphot.specio.write_fits_spec
documentation:
Write FITS spectrum.

:::{warning}
If data is being written out as single-precision but wavelengths
are in double-precision, some rows may be omitted.
:::

# Parameters

filename

: Output spectrum filename.

wavelengths, fluxes

: Wavelength and flux of the spectrum.

pri_header, ext_header

: Metadata to be added to primary and given extension FITS header,
  respectively. Do *not* use this to define column names and units.

overwrite

: Overwrite existing file. Defaults to `False`.

trim_zero

: Remove rows with zero-flux. Default is `True`.

pad_zero_ends

: Pad each end of the spectrum with a row of zero flux
  like {func}`synphot.spectrum.BaseSpectrum.taper`.
  This is unnecessary if input is already tapered.

precision

: Precision of values in output file.
  Use native flux precision by default.

epsilon

: Single-precision $\epsilon$ value, taken from IRAF SYNPHOT FAQ.
  This is the minimum separation in wavelengths necessary for SYNPHOT
  to read the entries as distinct single-precision numbers.
  This is *only* used if `precision='single'` but data are in
  double-precision. Default from the FAQ is 0.00032.

wave_col, flux_col

: Wavelength and flux column names (case-insensitive).

wave_unit, flux_unit

: Wavelength and flux units, which default to Angstrom and FLAM,
  respectively. These are *only* used if wavelengths and fluxes
  are not in astropy quantities.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Wavelengths and fluxes have difference shapes or value precision
  is not supported.

modules.name: synphot.filter_parameterization.filter_fft
documentation: Handle Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) for filter parameterization.

functions.name: synphot.filter_parameterization.filter_fft.filter_to_fft
documentation:
Calculate filter parameters using FFT.

# Parameters

bp

: Filter to parameterize.

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for sampling.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `waveset` is used.

n_terms

: Number of FFT parameters to keep.

# Returns

n_lambda

: Number of elements in `wl`.

lambda_0

: Minimum value of `wl`.

delta_lambda

: Median delta wavelength.

tr_max

: Maximum value of `tr`.

fft_parameters

: List of complex values that are FFT parameters to keep.

functions.name: synphot.filter_parameterization.filter_fft.filter_from_fft
documentation:
Reconstruct a filter from given FFT parameters.
The inputs for this function can be obtained from {func}`filter_to_fft`.

# Parameters

n_lambda

: Number of elements in original wavelength array.

lambda_0

: Minimum value of original wavelength array.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.

delta_lambda

: Median delta wavelength of original wavelength array.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.

tr_max

: Maximum value of transmittance curve.
  If a Quantity, must be unitless.

fft_parameters

: List of complex values that are FFT parameters representing the
  filter transmittance curve.

# Returns

bp

: Reconstructed filter.

functions.name: synphot.filter_parameterization.filter_fft.analytical_model_from_fft
documentation:
Similar to {func}`filter_from_fft` except that this returns
an analytical model.

:::{note}
This model needs to be sampled using the full range of
wavelength. See <https://github.com/bmorris3/tynt/issues/9> .
:::

# Returns

astropy_model

: A compound model that consists of
  `~astropy.modeling.functional_models.Sine1D` models.

functions.name: synphot.filter_parameterization.filter_fft.filters_to_fft_table
documentation:
Run {func}`filter_to_fft` on a list of filters
and store results in a table.

# Parameters

filters_mapping

: Dictionary mapping human-readable filter name to its
  `~synphot.spectrum.SpectralElement` and wavelengths, if applicable.
  If the filter object has a valid `waveset`, just provide `None`
  for wavelengths; otherwise provide a Quantity array for sampling.
  For example:

  ```
  {'JOHNSON/V': (<SpectralElement ...>, None),
   'Flat': (<SpectralElement ...>, <Quantity [1000., ..., 9999.] Angstrom>)}
  ```

n_terms

: Number of FFT parameters to keep.

# Returns

fft_table

: Table storing FFT parameterization for the given filters.
  Use its `write` method to save it to file.

functions.name: synphot.utils.validate_wavelengths
documentation:
Check wavelengths for `synphot` compatibility.

Wavelengths must satisfy these conditions:

> - valid unit type, if given
> - no zeroes
> - monotonic ascending or descending
> - no duplicate values

# Parameters

wavelengths

: Wavelength values.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Wavelengths unit type is invalid.

synphot.exceptions.DuplicateWavelength

: Wavelength array contains duplicate entries.

synphot.exceptions.UnsortedWavelength

: Wavelength array is not monotonic.

synphot.exceptions.ZeroWavelength

: Negative or zero wavelength occurs in wavelength array.

functions.name: synphot.utils.generate_wavelengths
documentation:
Generate wavelength array to be used for spectrum sampling.

$$
minwave \le \lambda < maxwave
$$

# Parameters

minwave, maxwave

: Lower and upper limits of the wavelengths.
  These must be values in linear space regardless of `log`.

num

: The number of wavelength values.
  This is only used when `delta=None`.

delta

: Delta between wavelength values.
  When `log=True`, this is the spacing in log space.

log

: If `True`, the wavelength values are evenly spaced in log scale.
  Otherwise, spacing is linear.

wave_unit

: Wavelength unit. Default is Angstrom.

# Returns

waveset

: Generated wavelength set.

waveset_str

: Info string associated with the result.

functions.name: synphot.utils.merge_wavelengths
documentation:
Return the union of the two sets of wavelengths using
{func}`numpy.union1d`.

The merged wavelengths may sometimes contain numbers which are nearly
equal but differ at levels as small as 1e-14. Having values this
close together can cause problems down the line. So, here we test
whether any such small differences are present, with a small
difference defined as less than `threshold`. If a small
difference is present, the lower of the too-close pair is removed.

# Parameters

waveset1, waveset2

: Wavelength values, assumed to be in the same unit already.
  Also see {func}`~synphot.models.get_waveset`.

threshold

: Merged wavelength values are considered "too close together"
  when the difference is smaller than this number.
  The default is 1e-12.

# Returns

out_wavelengths

: Merged wavelengths. `None` if undefined.

functions.name: synphot.utils.download_data
documentation:
Download `synphot` data files to given root directory or
the `astropy` cache.
Download is skipped if a data file already exists.

:::{warning}
Downloading data to `astropy` cache only is not recommended
if you plan to provide a custom `synphot.cfg`.
:::

# Parameters

path_root

: Root directory for data files. If `None`, download to the `astropy`
  cache location instead of a specific directory.

verbose

: Print extra information to screen.

dry_run

: Go through the logic but skip the actual download.
  This would return a list of files that *would have been*
  downloaded without network calls. The sub-directories
  would still be created regardless.
  Use this option for debugging or testing.

# Raises

OSError

: Problem with directory.

# Returns

file_list

: A list of downloaded files.

Model and functions related to blackbody radiation.

This was `astropy.modeling.blackbody` module that was

:::

functions.name: synphot.blackbody.blackbody_nu
documentation:
Calculate blackbody flux per steradian, $B_{\nu}(T)$.

:::{note}
Use `numpy.errstate` to suppress Numpy warnings, if desired.
:::

:::{warning}
Output values might contain `nan` and `inf`.
:::

# Parameters

in_x

: Frequency, wavelength, or wave number.
  If not a Quantity, it is assumed to be in Hz.

temperature

: Blackbody temperature.
  If not a Quantity, it is assumed to be in Kelvin.

# Returns

flux

: Blackbody monochromatic flux in
  $erg \; cm^{-2} s^{-1} Hz^{-1} sr^{-1}$.

# Raises

ValueError

: Invalid temperature.

ZeroDivisionError

: Wavelength is zero (when converting to frequency).

functions.name: synphot.blackbody.blackbody_lambda
documentation:
Like {func}`blackbody_nu` but for $B_{\lambda}(T)$.

# Parameters

in_x

: Frequency, wavelength, or wave number.
  If not a Quantity, it is assumed to be in Angstrom.

temperature

: Blackbody temperature.
  If not a Quantity, it is assumed to be in Kelvin.

# Returns

flux

: Blackbody monochromatic flux in
  $erg \; cm^{-2} s^{-1} \mathring{A}^{-1} sr^{-1}$.

classes.name: synphot.blackbody.BlackBody1D

methods.name: synphot.blackbody.BlackBody1D.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate the model.

# Parameters

x

: Frequency at which to compute the blackbody. If no units are given,
  this defaults to Hz.

temperature

: Temperature of the blackbody. If no units are given, this defaults
  to Kelvin.

bolometric_flux

: Desired integral for the blackbody.

# Returns

y

: Blackbody spectrum. The units are determined from the units of
  `bolometric_flux`.

methods.name: synphot.blackbody.BlackBody1D.lambda_max
documentation: Peak wavelength when the curve is expressed as power density.

One dimensional blackbody model.

# Parameters

temperature

: Blackbody temperature.

bolometric_flux

: The bolometric flux of the blackbody (i.e., the integral over the
  spectral axis).

# Notes

Model formula:

> $$
> f(x) = \pi B_{\nu} f_{\text{bolometric}} / (\sigma  T^{4})
> $$

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from astropy import units as u
>>> from synphot.blackbody import BlackBody1D
>>> bb = BlackBody1D()
>>> bb(6000 * u.AA)  # doctest: +FLOAT_CMP
<Quantity 1.35853828e-15 erg / (Hz s cm2)>
```

```
.. plot::
    :include-source:

    import numpy as np
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    from astropy import units as u
    from astropy.visualization import quantity_support
    from synphot.blackbody import BlackBody1D
    from synphot.units import FLAM

    bb = BlackBody1D(temperature=5778*u.K)
    wav = np.arange(1000, 110000) * u.AA
    flux = bb(wav).to(FLAM, u.spectral_density(wav))

    with quantity_support():
        plt.figure()
        plt.semilogx(wav, flux)
        plt.axvline(bb.lambda_max.to_value(u.AA), ls='--')
        plt.show()
```

methods.name: synphot.thermal.ThermalSpectralElement.thermal_source
documentation:
Apply emissivity to an existing beam to produce a thermal
source spectrum (without optical counterpart).

Thermal source spectrum is calculated as follow:

> 1. Create a blackbody spectrum in PHOTLAM per square arcsec
>    with `temperature`.
> 2. Multiply the blackbody with `beam_fill_factor` and `self`.

# Returns

sp

: Thermal source spectrum.

methods.name: synphot.thermal.ThermalSpectralElement.from_file
documentation:
Creates a thermal spectral element from file.

:::{note}
Only FITS format is supported.
:::

# Parameters

filename

: Thermal spectral element filename.

temperature_key, beamfill_key

: Keywords in FITS *table extension* that store temperature
  (in Kelvin) and beam filling factor values.
  Beam filling factor is set to 1 if its keyword is missing.

kwargs

: Keywords acceptable by {func}`~synphot.specio.read_fits_spec`.

# Returns

th

: Empirical thermal spectral element.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid inputs.

Class to handle spectral element with associated thermal
properties.

This differs from `~synphot.spectrum.SpectralElement` in the
sense that it carries thermal parameters, i.e., temperature
and beam filling factor.

:::{note}
Use {func}`thermal_source` to apply its emissivity to an
existing beam.
:::

# Parameters

modelclass, kwargs

: See `~synphot.spectrum.BaseSpectrum`.

temperature

: Temperature. If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Kelvin.

beam_fill_factor

: Beam filling factor. If a Quantity, must be unitless.
  Defaults to 1.

functions.name: synphot.reddening.etau_madau
documentation:
Madau 1995 extinction for a galaxy at given redshift.
This is the Lyman-alpha prescription from the photo-z code BPZ.

The Lyman-alpha forest approximately has an effective
"throughput" which is a function of redshift and
rest-frame wavelength.
One would multiply the SEDs by this factor before
passing it through an instrument filter.

This approximation is from Footnote 3 of
{ref}`Madau et al. (1995) <synphot-ref-madau1995>`.
This is claimed accurate to 5%.
The scatter in this factor (due to different lines of sight)
is huge, as shown in Madau's Fig. 3 (top panel);
The figure's bottom panel shows a redshifted version of the
"exact" prescription.

# Parameters

wave

: Redshifted wavelength values.
  Non-redshifted wavelength is `wave / (1 + z)`.

z

: Redshift.

kwargs

: Equivalencies for unit conversion, see
  {func}`~synphot.units.validate_quantity`.

# Returns

extcurve

: Extinction curve to apply to the redshifted spectrum.
TODO: Find a better way to handle so many magic numbers.
See https://github.com/spacetelescope/synphot_refactor/issues/77

functions.name: synphot.units.convert_flux
documentation:
Perform conversion for {ref}`supported flux units <synphot-flux-units>`.

# Parameters

wavelengths

: Wavelength values. If not a Quantity, assumed to be in
  Angstrom.

fluxes

: Flux values. If not a Quantity, assumed to be in PHOTLAM.

out_flux_unit

: Output flux unit.

area

: Area that fluxes cover. If not a Quantity, assumed to be in
  $cm^{2}$. This value *must* be provided for conversions involving
  OBMAG and count, otherwise it is not needed.

vegaspec

: Vega spectrum that *must* be provided for conversions involving
  VEGAMAG, otherwise it is not needed. For instance, it can be
  obtained from {func}`~synphot.spectrum.SourceSpectrum.from_vega`.

# Returns

out_flux

: Converted flux values.

# Raises

astropy.units.UnitsError

: Conversion failed.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Area or Vega spectrum is not given when needed.

documentation: Flux conversion for PHOTLAM <-> X.

functions.name: synphot.units.validate_unit
documentation:
Validate unit.

To be compatible with existing SYNPHOT data files:

    * 'angstroms' and 'inversemicrons' are accepted although
      unrecognized by astropy units
    * 'transmission', 'extinction', and 'emissivity' are
      converted to astropy dimensionless unit

Parameters
----------
input_unit : str or `~astropy.units.Unit`
    Unit to validate.

Returns
-------
output_unit : `~astropy.units.Unit`
    Validated unit.

Raises
------
synphot.exceptions.SynphotError
    Invalid unit.
Utility functions #

Calculated binned wavelength centers, edges, and flux.

By contrast, the native waveset and flux should be considered
samples of a continuous function.

Thus, it makes sense to interpolate `self.waveset` and
`self(self.waveset)`, but not `binset` and `binflux`.

methods.name: synphot.observation.Observation.sample_binned
documentation:
Sample binned observation without interpolation.

To sample unbinned data, use `__call__`.

# Parameters

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for sampling.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `binset` is used.

flux_unit

: Flux is converted to this unit.
  If not given, internal unit is used.

kwargs

: Keywords acceptable by {func}`~synphot.units.convert_flux`.

# Returns

flux

: Binned flux in given unit.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.InterpolationNotAllowed

: Interpolation of binned data is not allowed.

methods.name: synphot.observation.Observation.binned_waverange
documentation:
Calculate the wavelength range covered by the given number
of pixels centered on the given central wavelengths of
`binset`.

# Parameters

cenwave

: Desired central wavelength.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.

npix

: Desired number of pixels, centered on `cenwave`.

kwargs

: Keywords accepted by {func}`synphot.binning.wave_range`.

# Returns

waverange

: Lower and upper limits of the wavelength range,
  in the unit of `cenwave`.

methods.name: synphot.observation.Observation.effective_wavelength
documentation:
Calculate {ref}`effective wavelength <synphot-formula-effwave>`.

# Parameters

binned

: Sample data in native wavelengths if `False`.
  Else, sample binned data (default).

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for sampling.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `self.waveset` or `binset` is used, depending
  on `binned`.

mode

: Flux is first converted to the unit below before calculation:

  > - 'efflerg' - FLAM
  > - 'efflphot' - PHOTLAM (deprecated)

# Returns

eff_lam

: Observation effective wavelength.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid mode.

methods.name: synphot.observation.Observation.effstim
documentation:
Calculate {ref}`effective stimulus <synphot-formula-effstim>`
for given flux unit.

# Parameters

flux_unit

: The unit of effective stimulus.
  COUNT gives result in count/s (see {meth}`countrate` for more
  options).
  If not given, internal unit is used.

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for sampling. This must be given if
  `self.waveset` is undefined for the underlying spectrum model(s).
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `self.waveset` is used.

area, vegaspec

: See {func}`~synphot.units.convert_flux`.

# Returns

eff_stim

: Observation effective stimulus based on given flux unit.

methods.name: synphot.observation.Observation.countrate
documentation:
Calculate {ref}`effective stimulus <synphot-formula-effstim>`
in count/s.

# Parameters

area

: Area that flux covers. If not a Quantity, assumed to be in
  $cm^{2}$.

binned

: Sample data in native wavelengths if `False`.
  Else, sample binned data (default).

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for sampling. This must be given if
  `self.waveset` is undefined for the underlying spectrum model(s).
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `self.waveset` or `binset` is used, depending
  on `binned`.

waverange

: Lower and upper limits of the desired wavelength range.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, the full range is used.

force

: If a wavelength range is given, partial overlap raises
  an exception when this is `False` (default). Otherwise,
  it returns calculation for the overlapping region.
  Disjoint wavelength range raises an exception regardless.

# Returns

count_rate

: Observation effective stimulus in count/s.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.DisjointError

: Wavelength range does not overlap with observation.

synphot.exceptions.PartialOverlap

: Wavelength range only partially overlaps with observation.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Calculation failed, including but not limited to NaNs in flux.

methods.name: synphot.observation.Observation.as_spectrum
documentation:
Reduce the observation to an empirical source spectrum.

An observation is a complex object with some restrictions
on its capabilities. At times, it would be useful to work
with the observation as a simple object that is easier to
manipulate and takes up less memory.

This is also useful for writing an observation as sampled
spectrum out to a FITS file.

# Parameters

binned

: Write out data in native wavelengths if `False`.
  Else, write binned data (default).

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for sampling.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `self.waveset` or `binset` is used, depending
  on `binned`.

# Returns

sp

: Empirical source spectrum.

This is an observed spectrum, where a source spectrum
has gone through a bandpass.

Usually, this is the end point of a chain of spectral
manipulation. It has extra attributes that deal with binning,
which is introduced by the detector.

# Parameters

spec

: Source spectrum.

band

: Bandpass.

binset

: Center of binned wavelengths.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, input `self.waveset` values are used.

force

: Force creation of an observation even when source spectrum
  and bandpass do not fully overlap:

  > - `None` or 'none' - Source must encompass bandpass (default)
  > - 'extrap' - Extrapolate source spectrum (this changes the
  >   underlying model of `spec` to always extrapolate,
  >   if applicable)
  > - 'taper' - Taper source spectrum

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.DisjointError

: Bandpass does not overlap with source spectrum.

synphot.exceptions.PartialOverlap

: Bandpass only partially overlaps with source spectrum
  when they must fully overlap.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid inputs.

synphot.exceptions.UndefinedBinset

: Missing binned wavelength set.

functions.name: synphot.binning.wave_range
documentation:
Calculate the wavelength range covered by the given number of pixels
centered on the given central wavelength of the given bins.

# Parameters

bins

: Wavelengths at bin centers, each centered on a pixel.
  Must be 1D array.

cenwave

: Desired central wavelength, in the same unit as `bins`.

npix

: Desired number of pixels, centered on `cenwave`.

mode

: Determines how the pixels at the edges of the wavelength range
  are handled. All the options, except 'none', will return
  wavelength range edges that correspond to pixel edges:

  > - 'round' - Wavelength range edges are the pixel edges
  >   and the range spans exactly `npix` pixels. An edge
  >   that falls in the center of a bin is rounded to the
  >   nearest pixel edge. This is the default.
  > - 'min' - Wavelength range is shrunk such that it includes
  >   an integer number of pixels and its edges fall on pixel
  >   edges. It may not span exactly `npix` pixels.
  > - 'max' - Wavelength range is expanded such that it
  >   includes an integer number of pixels and its edges fall
  >   on pixel edges. It may not span exactly `npix` pixels.
  > - 'none' - Exact wavelength range is returned. The edges
  >   may not correspond to pixel edges, but it covers exactly
  >   `npix` pixels.

# Returns

wave1, wave2

: Lower and upper limits of the wavelength range.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.OverlapError

: Given central wavelength is not within the given bins
  or the wavelength range would exceed the bin limits.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid inputs or calculation failed.

functions.name: synphot.binning.pixel_range
documentation:
Calculate the number of pixels within the given wavelength range
and the given bins.

# Parameters

bins

: Wavelengths at bin centers, each centered on a pixel.
  Must be 1D array.

waverange

: Lower and upper limits of the desired wavelength range,
  in the same unit as `bins`.

mode

: Determines how the pixels at the edges of the wavelength range
  are handled. All the options, except 'none', will return
  an integer number of pixels:

  > - 'round' - Wavelength range edges that fall in the middle
  >   of a pixel are counted if more than half of the pixel is
  >   within the given wavelength range. Edges that fall in
  >   the center of a pixel are rounded to the nearest pixel
  >   edge. This is the default.
  > - 'min' - Only pixels wholly within the given wavelength
  >   range are counted.
  > - 'max' - Pixels that are within the given wavelength range
  >   by any margin are counted.
  > - 'none' - The exact number of encompassed pixels,
  >   including fractional pixels, is returned.

# Returns

npix

: Number of pixels.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.OverlapError

: Given wavelength range exceeds the bounds of given bins.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid mode.

methods.name: synphot.spectrum.BaseSpectrum.integrate
documentation:
Perform integration.

When integration is not analytical and wavelengths are provided,
flux or throughput is first resampled.
This is useful when user wants to integrate at specific end points
or use custom spacing; In that case, user can pass in desired
sampling array generated with {func}`numpy.linspace`,
{func}`numpy.logspace`, etc.
If not provided, then `waveset` is used.

When integration is analytical, wavelengths provided are only used
if applicable for the particular model. If the model does not
support analytical integration, it will fall back to simple
trapezoid integration.

# Parameters

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for integration.
  This is ignored by analytical integration if not applicable.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `waveset` is used.

integration_type

: Defines how the integration is done, either by simple trapezoid
  integration or analytical formula. If `None`, the option is pulled
  from `synphot.config.conf.default_integrator`. If analytical
  integration is requested but no possible, trapezoid integration
  is done anyway.

flux_unit

: **This option is only available for source spectrum.**
  For trapezoid integration, flux is converted to this unit for
  sampling before integration. For analytical integration, while
  calculation is done differently, providing this option would
  result in equivalent behavior as trapezoid integration, where
  possible, for consistency. If not given, internal unit is used.

kwargs

: **This option is only available for source spectrum.**
  Other optional keywords besides `flux_unit` to `__call__`
  for sampling when integration type is not analytical.

# Returns

result

: Integrated result.

# Raises

NotImplementedError

: Invalid integration type.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: `waveset` is needed but undefined or cannot integrate
  natively in the given `flux_unit`.

methods.name: synphot.spectrum.BaseSourceSpectrum.normalize
documentation:
Renormalize the spectrum to the given Quantity and band.

:::{warning}
Redshift attribute (`z`) is reset to 0 in the normalized
spectrum even if `self.z` is non-zero.
This is because the normalization simply adds a scale
factor to the existing composite model.
This is confusing but should not affect the flux sampling.
:::

# Parameters

renorm_val

: Value to renormalize the spectrum to. If not a Quantity,
  assumed to be in internal unit.

band

: Bandpass to use in renormalization.

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for renormalization.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `self.waveset` is used.

force

: By default (`False`), renormalization is only done
  when band wavelength limits are within `self`
  or at least 99% of the flux is within the overlap.
  Set to `True` to force renormalization for partial overlap
  (this changes the underlying model of `self` to always
  extrapolate, if applicable).
  Disjoint bandpass raises an exception regardless.

area, vegaspec

: See {func}`~synphot.units.convert_flux`.

# Returns

sp

: Renormalized spectrum.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.DisjointError

: Renormalization band does not overlap with `self`.

synphot.exceptions.PartialOverlap

: Renormalization band only partially overlaps with `self`
  and significant amount of flux falls outside the overlap.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid inputs or calculation failed.

methods.name: synphot.spectrum.SpectralElement.check_overlap
documentation:
Check for wavelength overlap between two spectra.

Only wavelengths where `self` throughput is non-zero
are considered.

Example of full overlap:

```
|---------- other ----------|
   |------ self ------|
```

Examples of partial overlap:

```
|---------- self ----------|
   |------ other ------|

|---- other ----|
   |---- self ----|

|---- self ----|
   |---- other ----|
```

Examples of no overlap:

```
|---- self ----|  |---- other ----|

|---- other ----|  |---- self ----|
```

# Parameters

other : `BaseSpectrum`

wavelengths

: Wavelength values for integration.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in Angstrom.
  If `None`, `waveset` is used.

threshold

: If less than this fraction of flux or throughput falls
  outside wavelength overlap, the *lack* of overlap is
  *insignificant*. This is only used when partial overlap
  is detected. Default is 1%.

# Returns

result
: - 'full' - `self` coverage is within or same as `other`
  - 'partial_most' - Less than `threshold` fraction of
    `self` flux is outside the overlapping wavelength
    region, i.e., the *lack* of overlap is *insignificant*
  - 'partial_notmost' - `self` partially overlaps with
    `other` but does not qualify for 'partial_most'
  - 'none' - `self` does not overlap `other`

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid inputs.
